# Rocca Tile & Granite LLC — Full Information

> Licensed tile, granite, and remodeling contractor serving Chino, CA and the Inland Empire since 2004.

## Company Information

- Company: Rocca Tile & Granite LLC
- Also Known As: Elegant Kitchens & Bath
- Owner: Filiberto Reyes
- Phone: (909) 238-3188
- Email: roccatile@yahoo.com
- Location: Chino, CA 91710
- Website: https://rocca-tile.com
- License: CSLB #927459 (Active, California Contractors State License Board)
- Established: 2004
- Hours: Monday–Friday 8 AM – 5 PM, Saturday–Sunday Closed
- Languages: English, Spanish
- Licensed & Insured: Yes

## About

Rocca Tile & Granite LLC was founded in 2004 by Filiberto Reyes as a specialized tile and stone contractor in Chino, California. Over two decades, the company grew from residential tile installation into a full-service kitchen and bathroom remodeling firm. Today, Rocca Tile & Granite handles custom granite countertop fabrication, cabinet construction and refinishing, complete kitchen and bathroom renovations, flooring installation, and room additions. The company also operates under the Elegant Kitchens & Bath brand for full-scope remodeling projects. Filiberto remains hands-on — he is on the jobsite, not just managing from an office. The company holds an active California Contractors State License Board license (#927459), continuously maintained since 2004.

## Services

### Kitchen Remodeling
Complete kitchen transformations including countertops, cabinets, tile backsplashes, flooring, and layout redesigns. Full gut-and-rebuild capability. Custom cabinet construction and pre-made installation. Granite, quartz, and marble countertop fabrication and installation. Handles plumbing and electrical coordination as a licensed general contractor. Typical cost: $15,000–$60,000. Timeline: 4–8 weeks.

### Bathroom Remodeling
Custom tile showers, granite vanities, tub-to-shower conversions, freestanding tub installations, and full bathroom renovations. Waterproofing, frameless glass enclosures, shower niches, and bench seats. Bathroom additions with full permitting. Typical cost: $8,000–$35,000. Timeline: 2–4 weeks.

### Tile Installation
Professional tile installation for floors, walls, showers, backsplashes, fireplace surrounds, and outdoor patios. All tile types: ceramic, porcelain, natural stone (travertine, marble, slate), mosaic, glass, 3D textured panels, and large-format tile. Indoor and outdoor. Specialty: intricate mosaic patterns and accent walls. Typical cost: $8–$25 per square foot installed.

### Granite Countertops
Custom countertop fabrication and installation working directly with local stone fabricators. Granite, quartz, marble, and natural stone. Kitchen and bathroom countertops, islands, and vanities. Undermount sink cutouts, edge profiling, seam placement. Template-to-install process. Typical cost: $40–$100 per square foot installed. Timeline: 7–10 business days from template to install.

### Cabinet Services
Full range of cabinet services: custom cabinet construction from scratch, pre-made cabinet installation, cabinet painting, refinishing, refacing, and add-ons. Kitchen cabinets, bathroom vanities, built-in closet systems, media centers. All styles: shaker, flat-panel, raised-panel, open shelving, glass-front. Typical cost: custom $500–$1,200 per linear foot; pre-made $150–$400 per linear foot; refacing $4,000–$10,000; refinishing $3,000–$7,000.

### Flooring Installation
All flooring types: tile, hardwood, luxury vinyl plank (LVP), laminate, carpet, and epoxy. Indoor and outdoor (patio tile, garage epoxy). Subfloor preparation, old flooring removal, leveling, moisture barriers included. Large-format tile, decorative inlay patterns, staircase treads. Typical cost: $5–$20 per square foot installed.

## Service Area

Based in Chino, CA with a 75-mile service radius covering:

### Inland Empire
Chino, Chino Hills, Ontario, Rancho Cucamonga, Upland, Fontana, Montclair, Eastvale, Corona, Norco, Riverside

### San Gabriel Valley
Pomona, Diamond Bar, Walnut, West Covina, Covina, Azusa, Glendora, La Verne, Claremont, San Dimas, Arcadia, Pasadena, Rowland Heights, Whittier

### North Orange County
Brea, Yorba Linda, Placentia, Fullerton, Anaheim, Anaheim Hills, La Habra

### Eastern Los Angeles County
Rowland Heights, Hacienda Heights, La Puente, Baldwin Park, El Monte, Irwindale

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How much does a kitchen remodel cost in Chino?
A: Kitchen remodels in the Chino and Inland Empire area typically range from $15,000 to $60,000 depending on scope. A basic refresh (new countertops, backsplash, cabinet refacing) runs $15,000–$25,000. A full gut-and-rebuild with custom granite, new cabinetry, and flooring usually falls between $35,000 and $60,000.

Q: How long does a bathroom remodel take?
A: Most bathroom remodels take 2 to 4 weeks depending on scope. A straightforward tile and vanity replacement can be completed in 10 to 14 days. Full bathroom renovations with layout changes typically require 3 to 4 weeks.

Q: Do you fabricate granite countertops in-house?
A: We work directly with local stone fabricators to template, cut, and finish countertops to exact specifications. Template-to-install typically takes 7–10 business days.

Q: What types of tile do you install?
A: All types: ceramic, porcelain, natural stone (travertine, marble, slate), mosaic, glass, 3D textured panels, and large-format tile. Indoor and outdoor installations.

Q: Are you a licensed contractor?
A: Yes. Rocca Tile & Granite LLC holds an active California Contractors State License Board (CSLB) license #927459, continuously maintained since 2004. Fully licensed and insured.

Q: What areas do you serve?
A: We serve a 75-mile radius from Chino, CA, covering the Inland Empire, San Gabriel Valley, North Orange County, and parts of eastern Los Angeles County.

Q: Can you handle an entire kitchen remodel or do I need separate contractors?
A: We handle everything in-house — demolition, cabinets, countertops, tile, flooring, and fixture installation. As a licensed general contractor, we coordinate plumbing and electrical work so you have one point of contact.

Q: What's the most durable flooring for a kitchen?
A: Porcelain tile and luxury vinyl plank (LVP) are the top choices. Tile is waterproof, scratch-resistant, and lasts decades. LVP is waterproof, softer underfoot, and installs faster. We install both.

Q: Do you offer free estimates?
A: Yes. We provide free on-site estimates with detailed, itemized pricing for all services.

Q: What languages do you speak?
A: English and Spanish.
